theme: power-grid load shedding under rolling line trips
title: Blackstart Triage: Proactive Load Shedding under Rolling Line Trips
innovation_hook: Shed proactively along the predicted cascade frontier rather than at the present hotspot, keeping every redistribution below neighbor margins.
strategies: do-nothing baseline: never shed; the whole cascade is absorbed or lost with no intervention (reproduces the evaluator's do-nothing reference, ~0.1)

hotspot relief (the trap): each step shed the currently-most-utilised live line down to a safe utilisation; ignores the public trip schedule, so it wastes the small shed budget on high-utilisation decoys and eats the corridor cascade

schedule-aware frontier shedding (the insight): read the public trip schedule, identify the live neighbours the next trip will hit, and PRE-shed exactly those frontier lines across the steps leading up to the trip (latest-first, within the per-step budget) so the incoming redistribution stays below their margin and the runaway cascade never starts

full multi-step planner: additionally pre-shed the scheduled line itself to shrink the wave, and optimise the timing/placement of curtailment against the convex carryover cost across multiple waves and cross-corridor bridges
